title: error resolving mountpoints for container: write /proc/thread-self/attr/sockcreate: permission denied
body:
Seen in [gating tests](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/44f7a614-b253-4717-8022-ff8950a9c97e/) (rawhide, per Lokesh):
```
not ok 697 bud with ssh key
# (from function `die' in file /usr/share/buildah/test/system/helpers.bash, line 314,
#  from function `run_buildah' in file /usr/share/buildah/test/system/helpers.bash, line 301,
#  in test file /usr/share/buildah/test/system/ssh.bats, line 24)
#   `run_buildah bud --ssh default=$mytmpdir/sshkey $WITH_POLICY_JSON  -t sshimg -f $BUDFILES/run-mounts/Dockerfile.ssh $BUDFILES/run-mounts' failed
# /usr/share/buildah/test/system /var/str
# # [checking for: docker.io/library/alpine]
# # [restoring from cache: /var/tmp/buildah-image-cache.5434 / docker.io/library/alpine]
# Getting image source signatures
# Copying blob sha256:9d16cba9fb961d1aafec9542f2bf7cb64acfc55245f9e4eb5abecd4cdc38d749
# Copying config sha256:961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4
# Writing manifest to image destination
# Storing signatures
# $ /usr/bin/buildah bud --ssh default=/var/tmp/buildah_tests.qezr9z/my-dir1/sshkey --signature-policy /usr/share/buildah/test/system/policy.json -t sshimg -f /usr/share/buildah/test/system/bud/run-mounts/Dockerfile.ssh /usr/share/buildah/test/system/bud/run-mounts
# STEP 1/3: FROM alpine
# STEP 2/3: RUN apk add openssh-client
# fetch http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/main/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
# fetch http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.10/community/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
# (1/5) Installing openssh-keygen (8.1_p1-r0)
# (2/5) Installing ncurses-terminfo-base (6.1_p20190518-r2)
# (3/5) Installing ncurses-libs (6.1_p20190518-r2)
# (4/5) Installing libedit (20190324.3.1-r0)
# (5/5) Installing openssh-client (8.1_p1-r0)
# Executing busybox-1.30.1-r2.trigger
# OK: 10 MiB in 19 packages
# STEP 3/3: RUN --mount=type=ssh,id=default ssh-add -l -E md5
# error building at STEP "RUN --mount=type=ssh,id=default ssh-add -l -E md5": error resolving mountpoints for container "9c75ee7408e1bffdacd5e86ded060f150eae17d12a988712cd7095dbd3dbdb7b": write /proc/thread-self/attr/sockcreate: permission denied
# [ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
I'm merely assuming that it's a flake (I have no way to re-run this test to find out).

comments.body:
I am not at all sure but it looks like buildah is setting socket label incorrectly withot acquiring lock, maybe happening just when we start serving `ssh-agent` , although i found one issue in buildah which could have direct or indirect effect on this issue, we must acquire `LockOSThread` before setting the socket label.

I'll open a PR for this issue. @edsantiago How often do we see this flake ?

I have not seen this flake in Cirrus CI. The only instance is the one I reported, which is rawhide gating tests. That's what makes it a little more pressing: we don't test rawhide in CI, so there could be a problem that we don't yet know about.

The `attr/sockcreate` is actually set while we `setSocketLabel` ( https://github.com/opencontainers/selinux/blob/main/go-selinux/selinux_linux.go#L738 ), however I see no reference in lib for `EPERM` in case of thread race but I think its coming from lower layer and following PR should fix it: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4329
